reviews.review_text: the fighting of this game has been set absolutely to perfection you get your limbs cut off then thrown back at you, and lose your limbs to horse dung being flung at you by a catapult. Absolutely an amazing game 10/10

Why do people like cheering for FC Barcalona or Manchester City? Because most people are simpletons and therefore love teams that win all the time. I love this game but it is marred by imbalanced maps whcih in turn encourages stat chasing. Each map is basically Manchester City vs Burnley or FC Barcelona vs Malaga. 

The game is designed to give an advantage for the attackers. This has somewhat logical reasons since it allows the player base to experience the entirety of the stages in each level, with the final one being the most dramatic - I get tingles down my spine when I hear that dramatic music at the end stage. However, this bias by design has inevitably led to team stacking with higher level players joining the attacking team en masse (doesn't matter if Mason or Agatha) since being assault is "the most fun" as you basically steam roll the defense stage after stage. I don't recall if spawn times are shorter but the spawn points are definitely closer to the objective for the attackers. The final stage is supposed to be the most difficult for the attackers but by that time the attacking team has accumulated so much added bonus time that it is often a forgone conclusion. Yes, sometimes Burnely or Malaga beat the giants, which makes the victory all the more savory, but it cannot quite wash down the bitter taste of defeat, time after time.

I'm sure the developers have data that illustrates this. This might suggest the creation of the Montreux map, whose portcullis stage (cranking the gates open) is extremely difficult and greatly favors the defense. This was an over correction in my opinion. From my experience, when this map comes up, the bandwagon attacking veterans players either quit the server or actually join the defense because the map is clearly in their favor. 

Are there solutions? I hope so in Chivalry 3. Until then, perhaps include some kind of a penalty when switching teams because ultimately, this is what I believe the team stackers are after, admiring their own stats while playing on easy mode.
lazy and problematic devs left this good game to die for a ♥♥♥♥♥♥ zombie game and lead the best chiv 2 youtuber to su*cide. Don't support these devs. RIP SOTER DAVE

There are very few games I play that I get wrecked more often than I win, and of those few its exceedingly rare that I enjoy the game. (This isn't meant a brag, just to put some perspective on this review)

That said, I get destroyed almost every game lol, most of the time I die more than I kill, sometimes I'll go 0-5, I'll get a bunch of takedowns along the way, but usually die to a spear in the back or some crap.

But man, is it fun lmao. I just go in with the mindset of I want my team to win the game and these stats mean nothing to me, because this is not a genre of game I play, and I have laughed harder at some of the random **** I have seen happen, than I have at any other game in quite awhile.

And the few times Ive managed to not only survive, but win a 1v2 have felt crazy epic 😂.

If I went in thinking I needed to go 25-5 every game like I can in battlefield, I would rage quit this game so quick it would make your head spin lmao, but as long as you go in, knowing the game is a brawler style chaos fighting game, and you can still help your team win even if you don't technically score kills (takedowns ftw lol) this game is a good time.

Fun, but a lower player count game. Crappy devs and game should've been more and with modding support but you can still find a lobby. Really the only game of the genre other than mordhau which has less than 1k players atm. At least free weekend, game pass, and console version keeps some new players coming in. 

Don't pay more than $5-8 for this though

Servers are broken and have been for almost a week. Just in time for a sale woohooo! The developers dropped this game like a sack of rocks after their Epic 1 year exclusive money dried up. Chiv 2 feels worse than the first game to me and the introduction of the crappy tenosians and their maps being forced on you for half the maps in the game is ridiculous. I bought this game to play as a knight not some dude in cloth doing taliban screams.  Torn Banner Studios loves introducing crap no one wants or asked for in their games then when they kill of their player count they ditch the games. The forced slowdown on your swings is so dumb and makes it feel like you are fighting underwater and I know at least half of the players in top ranks have modded that crap out because honestly its a dumb mechanic.

Combat is good, but the team objective game mode (unless you are into dueling, this is what you will play the most) is extremely unfair and frustrating after a while. You will be playing defense at least 50% of the time unless you switch teams, and playing defense sucks.

As a defender in a team objective match, you will, by design, lose most of the fights due to spawn positions and timing (attackers will overwhelm you with closer spawns and shorter spawn times). The game wants the defenders to lose all but the last stage of the objective, and that's not fun for defenders. You can mitigate this by picking your fights and running away, but that's just a less fun way to play. 

Experienced players know the issue with defense, and some will switch to attack when given the chance, worsening the problem.

If the game modes were better, I would have played even more. Most of the time, I end a session after a frustrating match on defense. In many cases, I can predict how much fun I'll have based solely on which team I'm randomly assigned to at the start of the match, and that's not a good player experience.

I would give Chivalry 2 a middle thumb if that option existed.  The combat mechanics are excellent and absolutely fun.  Some of the game modes and design decisions are less than fun.  I play the game only for the group combat, and begrudgingly, some of the game modes - if lucky enough to queue into a fun map.  Some of the game modes are awesome/epic.  Others are designed with clunky objectives that seem to slightly favor one faction.  You cannot queue as your favorite faction.  You may also be auto-moved to the opposing team in the middle of a match.  In summary, "wack" objectives, the inability to consistently play as your favored faction, and the chance to be moved teams mid-game are an unpleasant combination.  I hope Chivalry 3 improves on this game.

I have hundreds of hours on xbox, and I will always recommend this game. It's chaotic fun where you can look at pig's glorified butthole. Truly great and should be given a try! The game I mean... Not the pig's butthole. Ew

As a casual game, it’s huge fun with tons of chaos. 
Competitively, it has a combat system with good depth to explore.

Learning the combat is extremely rewarding. My K/D went positive in private servers and infinite in matchmaking ~lvl 300. I started winning 1v12+ fights in TDM ~lvl 600. I can log in any day and have duels unmatched by anything I’ve experienced in other action games.

Community is also great. People train each other and organize events. The occasional cheats are easy to handle.
The game is finalized, so weapon balances and bugs are here to stay.

However, if they had added new maps and new classes, it could still be one of the all-time best games with hundreds of thousands of players.

So if anyone is familiar with it, there is a game called MORDHAU. If you have played that game, this game resolves its issues. It gets rid of the absurdly high skill ceiling so you can just play the game. Main issue I have with the game is monetization. They have multiple campaigns that are basically battle passes you need to progress through to earn cosmetics, and you need to pay ten dollars per campaign to fully unlock it, which wouldn't be a problem but the game already costs money and progression is insanely slow. There's also cosmetics that can ONLY be unlocked by grinding through a PAID campaign, which is ridiculous. With how much they monetize the game you'd expect the ability to be able to just buy the cosmetics you want, but no. Monetization issues aside, the game is tons of fun, whether you play it alone or with friends. It's combat is much more beginner friendly than MORDHAU and as a result you can reach average skill level quite quickly. My only gripe gameplaywise is that there's no enemy horde mode to let you kind of just turn your brain off. Overall rating 7/10. If there were no monetization issues it'd be a 9/10 but the monetization seriously holds the game back.
I love this game, I wish there was a bigger community. I don't understand how/ why it doesn't have more players.

Excellent game. You will be ganked for the first 10-20 hours. Then you may get fed up and watch a guide on YouTube. 
I can tell you I'm still getting ganked, but I now know why.

Chivalry 2 is a perfect representation of medieval warfare! The game has a bit of a learning curve to it and when your matchmaking the teams are majority experienced players so it can be hard to get into. That being said, if your willing to put in the effort to learn how to play the game efficiently enough so your not getting stomped on every fight you participate in (especially 1v1 battles) the game is quite enjoyable!
This game chivalry 2 really just tickles my toes when i play, Sometimes ill be having a bad day and ill load up some chivalry 2 to make it worse.

Game is great and is up there with being a good slasher on the market (not many to compete with)
I found this more enjoyable over the other slashers due to the better animation i.e. better than Mordhau animations (still not good and can be abused)
Was abandoned before needed balance changes
Some report cheaters (I personally don't see any so I'm lucky)

Core aspects of the game that allow for smooth group fights are; one not explained very well, or two broken/bugged
For example the active riposte which works 60% of the time but is crucial to fight multiple people
You either riposte or parry then look at your opponent to get active block/counter frames in front of you
This lasts up to I believe 2 seconds if you riposte
This mechanic is finnicky and I would argue doesn't work as intended
Doesn't trigger when riposting and looking at opponents or their swings

None of this matters as this is still a fun game that itches the hack and slash market with very little games to offer the same
I hope more learn from their predecessors and attempt this game genre again
Don't even try to get into this game atp, its fun for like 10 hours then its just constant max level people who never dropped the game. just get it if its on sale.
this game have lot of cheater especially the one using chinese and japanese name, not recommend it
best medieval game I’ve played. The battles are hilarious, but still rewarding if you actually know what you’re doing.. not just random sword swinging (still fun if you just swing)

I have 3000 hours on this game  on epic. This game is full of hackers. Regardless of how much you practice and learn you will never be able to compete with people using these hacks so don't waste your time. They use hacks that read incoming attacks and automatically parry and block so they never lose stamina. They kill everyone in the servers automatically and crash the servers. When the hacks were exposed on youtube they banned the youtubers account and he later killed himself. Do not pay for this game. The devs abandoned this game before releasing a 3rd installment.

Very ganky combat, not tight at all. Super unpolished to the point that even basic game mechanics don't work as intended. Honestly for how bad it is, it makes For Honor look good. Even the hitboxes don't work correctly.

Discord staff is on a power trip. I got warned for sharing a video of a cheater, then when i brought it up in the server i got banned. All i did was post the video, it got deleted, then I tagged someone I was discussing the cheater problem with and said I got warned for it. I had no prior warnings or issues.

Whilst Chivalry 2 is innately a great game mechanically, and has wonderful maps that're easily memorable, I sadly have to write a negative review. The community is becoming a stressful, rampant problem. Racism, anti-semitic comments, and mob harassment by kicking those who call out these bad behaviors are front of the line issues this game is plagued by. 

I have tried to contact the devs via Discord, I was proceeded to be told to send screens of bad actors and report, but after a week of in-game reporting alone, none have seen consequences and actively seek people like me out to kick. I don't want to do even more diligence just to probably be given similar results. 

In their own Discord as well, I asked about these rampant issues, and similar bad actors told me to "ignore it" or that hate speech is "free speech", whilst the developer's in-game/Discord TOS explicitly states this is a violation of the contractual agreement signed. 

All of these issues, have been existent for 4+ years and to this date, still exist.

Ultimately, I can not recommend this to anyone looking to play, as I have found that the chat in-game is part of the experience when toxicity isn't the main flagship of the lobby, and as stated above it is problematic. If you can play without chat, I would recommend it then. 

Please rate this review helpful if you have similar experiences, as it makes it rise to the top and causes sales issues for the developer, who do not want to uphold their own ruleset.
